title: melastoma malabathricum subsp. normale
intro: Melastoma malabathricum subsp. normale, also known as the Malaysian primrose or Malabar melastome, is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. The plant is traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine for its various medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Melastoma malabathricum subsp. normale include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is commonly consumed as a tea or taken orally for its medicinal benefits.
